calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carroll Collins appeared by zoom for Tristan Harvey.

Mr. Collins stated that Mr. Harvey is not present, that they last spoke on December 30th and that Mr. Harvey informed him that he (Mr. Harvey) was to report to the Sheridan Correctional Facility on Monday January 4th. The Court stated that it understands that Counsel has done his best to manage this matter and his Client. The Court also informed Mr. Collins that it will take the matter under submission, and that it will not approve his Client's actions, but it can get this matter closed. Mr. Collins stated that his Client transferred funds to himself without him knowing, that it didn't hurt anybody and that it was his Client's money. Mr. Collins also stated that he did not request fees for the work that was actually done and that it was higher than the statutory fees. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: S. Park. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Continued To Jan-06-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-28-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Continued To Sep-01-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carroll Collins appeared by zoom for Tristan Harvey (Executor).

Mr. Collins stated that they have the inventory and appraisal, that it has been signed by the Probate Referee, and that they need to get it signed by the Client and file it. Mr. Collins also stated that there is an issue of back taxes, that he is requesting 30-days, that he did not file the order to lift suspension, that he spoke with his Client yesterday and that his Client said he would sign the inventory and appraisal. The Court informed Mr. Collins that if the inventory and appraisal is not filed, the Executor will be suspended. Status on close of estate is continued to September 1, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Aug-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-17-2020 Text Ruling

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-17-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-17-2020 Continued To May-12-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Carroll J. Collins appeared by telephone (the Dept. 204 line) for and with Tristan Harvey (Administrator) who was present in the courtroom.

The Court stated that we were here on two matters and inquired if Mr. Harvey was serious about getting this done. Mr. Harvey confirmed for the Court that he is serious about getting the estate closed. The Court stated that at his point that it would lift the Orders to Appear and would reinstate Mr. Harvey. The Court inquired about the inventory and appraisal. Mr. Collins stated that his declaration stated that he had been unable to contact his client, that he received the inventory and appraisal from Frank Mahoney, that he hasn't submitted it to the court for filing because his client was suspended, that his client could not sign it, and that once signed he would be filing it with the court. The Court stated that Inheritance Funding Company is an assignee not a creditor and that they will not be paid until final distribution. The Court continued the status on close of the estate to May 12, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the filing of either a petition for final distribution or a petition to approve further report on status of administration. The orders to appear are off calendar. Mr. Collins is to prepare a form of order lifting the suspension of Tristan Harvey, reinstating him, and reflecting the Court's continance to May 12, 2020. The inventory and appraisal is to be filed. The Court noted that if either petition is on file one week in advance of the May 12th status hearing that it would be taken off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Continued To Mar-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Continued To Mar-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carroll Collins for Tristan Harvey.
Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company.

Mr. Collins stated that he last spoke with his Client on January 16th, that he told his Client that it was necessary to get things done, that they agreed to meet, but Mr. Harvey did not appear on the 17th, that he called Mr. Harvey on February 7th, February 11th and February 13th, but his message box was full, that he e-mailed Mr. Harvey twice and that he wrote Mr. Harvey a letter on February 14th. Mr. Collins also stated that his Client is the sole beneficiary of his Father's estate, that the estate is potentailly complicated, that the Father was the creator of Burning Man and that he has tried diligently to get Mr. Harvey to sign an inventory and appraisal. In addition, Mr. Collins stated that his Client received a $1,000,000.00 bank account, that he is very frustrated, that he has tried very hard to bring some clarity to this matter and that he told his Client that he needed to be here. Mr. Schroeder expressed concern, and asked that the Court issue a citation as to why Mr. Harvey should not be held in contempt and/or issue a bench warrant. Mr. Collins stated that there is no real property, that the main asset is cash, that his Client believes that his Father's papers have value and that nothing has been provided to the Referee. Ms. Schroeder stated that when they became involved, they were shown a bank statement of $1,400,000.00, and raised the issue of perhaps having the Public Administrator get involved. Status on close of estate and the order to appear are continued to March 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court suspends Tristan Harvey as the Court appointed Fiduciary. An order to show cause as to why Mr. Harvey should not be removed will be issued with a return date of March 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dpet. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-21-2020 Continued To Feb-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on February 18, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why the inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
